DISCOVERY 610, APPEARANCE: SUSAN D. CONDON, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF SHOOK, HARDY & BACON, LLP. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL THE ATTENDANCE AT DEPOSITION IN SAN FRANCISCO OF DALE CHAFFIN AS ONE OF THE PERSONS MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE IS DENIED. SECTION 2025(D)(6) OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE PROVIDES THAT THE DEPONENT SHALL DESIGNATE AND PRODUCE THE PERSON OR PERSONS MOST QUALIFIED TO TESTIFY AS TO SUBJECT #4 OF THE DEPOSITION NOTICE. THE COURT CANNOT DETERMINE AT THIS TIME WHETHER LINDA STURGEON LACKS SUFFICIENT KNOWLEDGE OF THE REQUESTED INFORMATION SO AS TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO PRODUCE ANOTHER INDIVIDUAL PURSUANT TO SECTION 2025(E)(2) OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN

MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Nicholas B. Waranoff for

APPEARANCES:
Nicholas B. Waranoff for defendants and moving party
NO OTHER APPEARANCE. HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion to compel answers to deposition questions:
Neil Sprague
The motion is granted. The Court does not find an express waiver of the attorney client privilege based on the prior deposition testimony of Albert Chaffin. However, the Court finds an implied waiver of the privilege based on the witness acting as both counsel and negotiator of the lease agreement and plaintiff's claim of reliance upon fraudulent representations by defendants as a basis for entering into the lease as well as material omissions of fact. Plaintiff's knowledge of any representations made by defendants during negotiations and the reasonableness of such reliance are proper subjects of discovery.
Albert Chaffin
The motion is granted. The Court does not find an express waiver of the attorney client privilege based on the prior deposition testimony. The testimony did not disclose a significant part of the communication within the meaning of Evidence Code Section 912. However, the Court finds an implied waiver of the privilege for the above stated reasons.
Patrick McLarney
The motion is granted. The objections based on Rifkind v. Superior Court (1994) 22 Cal.App. 4th 1255 are overruled. The witness may be examined as to his knowledge regarding the contents and the subject matter of the lease and his understanding of relevant provisions.
The request for monetary sanctions is denied.
Deposition to resume at time and place qgreed by parties. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
